text:
One afternoon in the quiet town of Eldergrove, a teenage girl named Lily woke up feeling unusually scared. She didn didn't know why. It was just a quiet summer day. The birds chirped. Butterflies danced. The breeze rustled leaves. Even the moon above seemed out just playing hide-and-seek.

Lily climbed out of bed, her heart pounding. She looked out her window. She imagined all sorts of creepy crawlies scurrying under the curtains. She could see the moonlight dancing across a field. Her imagination, it seemed, had a terrible sense of humor.

Determined to chase the shadows away, Lily grabbed her flashlight and decided to investigate the strange noises. She tiptoed across her bedroom rug, careful not to wake her tiny kitty. Outside, the shadows seemed to be getting larger as she moved.

With each step she took, something whispered behind her. There was nothing THERE. Her breathing steadied. The whispering vanished. That's when
